reforms.description: The draft bill for the Research Data Act aims to facilitate the access, linkage, and processing of sensitive data by both private and public institutions for research purposes, reducing administrative burdens for firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhances data accessibility for research, fostering innovation and collaboration between public and private sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: The draft bill for the Research Data Act aims to facilitate access to sensitive data.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform addresses significant barriers to data access, which is crucial for enhancing research capabilities and innovation in Germany.
reforms.source_quote: The draft bill for the Research Data Act aims to facilitate the access, linkage and processing of sensitive data by both private and public institutions for research purposes.

reforms.description: Accelerating the digitalisation of the public sector and upskilling civil servants is expected to improve spending efficiency and reduce administrative burdens, while also fostering the development of innovative AI solutions.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhances the skills of civil servants, leading to more efficient public services and better support for innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: Accelerating the digitalisation of the public sector and upskilling civil servants could significantly raise spending efficiency.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative addresses critical skills gaps in the public sector, which is essential for leveraging technology and improving service delivery.
reforms.source_quote: Accelerating the digitalisation of the public sector and upskilling civil servants could significantly raise spending efficiency and reduce the administrative burden.

reforms.description: Germany aims to foster regional development by better coordinating place-based policies with industrial, infrastructure, and innovation policies, while enhancing the financial and administrative capacity of municipalities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improved coordination of policies is expected to enhance regional economic dynamism and support innovation in local economies.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative was outlined as a key recommendation for fostering regional development.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant effort to align various policy areas to enhance regional development, which is crucial for maintaining economic stability.
reforms.source_quote: Fostering regional development requires better coordination of place-based with industrial, infrastructure and innovation policies.

reforms.description: Germany plans to raise public investment in infrastructure and R&D to enhance economic growth and address structural challenges in the economy.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased public investment in R&D is expected to boost innovation and productivity, leading to higher potential GDP growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has announced plans to raise public investment in infrastructure and R&D.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant commitment to enhancing public investment in critical areas that support long-term economic growth and innovation.
reforms.source_quote: Raising public investment in infrastructure and R&D is crucial for addressing structural challenges and reviving economic growth.

reforms.description: The German government plans to raise depreciation allowances for equipment investments from 2025-27, aiming to stimulate business investment and enhance productivity.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By incentivizing firms to invest in equipment, this reform is expected to enhance productivity and support economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has announced plans to raise depreciation allowances for equipment investments.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it directly targets business investment, which is crucial for economic recovery and growth.
reforms.source_quote: The government plans to raise depreciation allowances for equipment investments from 2025-27.

reforms.description: The government intends to lower the corporate income tax by one percentage point per year over five years starting in 2028, aimed at improving the investment climate.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Lowering corporate taxes is expected to enhance firms' profitability and incentivize further investments, thus supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has announced plans to lower the corporate income tax starting in 2028.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it aims to improve the overall investment climate, which is crucial for economic recovery.
reforms.source_quote: The government plans to lower the corporate income tax from 2028 by one percentage point per year over a period of five years.

reforms.description: The federal government has strengthened public co-financing of venture capital through the Growth and Innovation Capital for Germany programme, which aims to mobilise EUR 12 billion to secure a total of EUR 30 billion in innovative start-up financing by 2030.
reforms.package_name: Growth and Innovation Capital for Germany
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By increasing access to venture capital, the reform supports the growth of innovative start-ups, which can enhance productivity and competitiveness in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme was launched in September 2024 to address the funding gap for start-ups.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in the financing landscape for start-ups, aiming to mobilise substantial private investment and enhance the overall innovation ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: To address the funding gap for start-ups, the federal government and its development bank have strengthened public co-financing of venture capital.

reforms.description: The Future Financing Act simplifies the requirements for initial public offerings by reducing the minimum market capitalisation and introducing dual-class shares, which aims to attract more venture capital investment in innovative start-ups.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By lowering barriers to public listings, the reform encourages investment in innovative firms, enhancing their growth potential and access to capital.
reforms.status_evidence: The Future Financing Act has been enacted to simplify IPO requirements.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in the regulatory framework for public offerings, which is crucial for enhancing the startup ecosystem and attracting venture capital.
reforms.source_quote: The Future Financing Act simplifies the requirements for initial public offerings by reducing the minimum market capitalisation from EUR 1.25 million to EUR 1 million.

reforms.description: A recent bill aims to foster viable exit options for innovative start-ups by facilitating reinvestment in corporate equity, raising the cap on the transfer of gains from the sale of shares in corporations, and reducing restrictions for private investment funds to increase their positions in venture capital funds.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing exit options for start-ups encourages investment and innovation, thereby supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The bill is set to be approved to enhance exit options for innovative start-ups.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant policy change aimed at improving the startup ecosystem, which is crucial for fostering innovation and economic dynamism.
reforms.source_quote: A recent bill aims at further fostering viable exit options for innovative start-ups and should be approved.

reforms.description: The Future Financing Act has been introduced to simplify the requirements for initial public offerings and improve conditions for institutional investors to invest in venture capital, fostering viable exit options for innovative start-ups.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing access to venture capital, the reform supports the growth and sustainability of innovative start-ups, which can drive economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The Future Financing Act has been enacted to improve conditions for venture capital investment.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant overhaul of the regulatory framework for venture capital, which is crucial for fostering innovation and supporting the start-up ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: The Future Financing Act aims at further improving conditions for institutional investors to invest in venture capital.

reforms.description: Germany plans to adopt and implement a research data act to facilitate policy impact evaluation and better target support measures in the future, addressing the need for rigorous evaluations on the effects of subsidies and tax expenditures on competition and innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving data access and evaluation capabilities can enhance the effectiveness of innovation policies and support targeted interventions.
reforms.status_evidence: It should be a key policy priority to adopt and implement the planned research data act.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform aims to enhance the evaluation of existing support measures, which is significant for improving the overall innovation policy framework.
reforms.source_quote: It should be a key policy priority to adopt and implement the planned research data act to facilitate policy impact evaluation and better target support measures in the future.

reforms.description: Germany has strengthened competition enforcement by introducing a market investigation tool that allows the competition authority to issue binding orders to firms to correct market distortions identified through sector-specific investigations.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Strengthening competition enforcement can foster innovation by ensuring a level playing field and preventing anti-competitive practices that hinder new entrants.
reforms.status_evidence: Competition enforcement has been recently strengthened by introducing a market investigation tool.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of a market investigation tool represents a significant structural change in competition enforcement, enhancing the ability to address market distortions effectively.
reforms.source_quote: Competition enforcement has been recently strengthened by introducing a market investigation tool.

reforms.description: Germany aims to improve digital, energy, innovation, and transport infrastructure to support regional development amidst structural changes. This includes better coordination of place-based policies with federal grants to enhance spending efficiency.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing infrastructure is expected to raise productivity and facilitate economic integration, thereby supporting regional growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Plans to improve infrastructure have been outlined as part of the regional development strategy.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform addresses critical infrastructure needs that can significantly impact regional economic performance, though it is part of a broader strategy.
reforms.source_quote: Improving digital, energy, innovation and transport infrastructure is key for regional development.

reforms.description: Germany's Federal Funding System for Structural Development regions aims to foster business activity and employment in disadvantaged regions by providing investment grants, wage subsidies, and support for research and innovation.
reforms.package_name: Federal Funding System for Structural Development
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By targeting investment in research and innovation, the funding system aims to enhance productivity and economic activity in underperforming regions.
reforms.status_evidence: The federal government’s place-based policy framework aims at fostering business activity and employment in disadvantaged regions.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in how funding is allocated to support regional development, with a focus on innovation and economic activity.
reforms.source_quote: The GFS programmes provide investment grants, wage subsidies, below-market loans, support for research and innovation, employee training and digitalisation for firms.

reforms.description: A 2022 reform linked wage subsidies to high-skilled employment and removed the minimum distance eligibility condition for trade in goods and services, aiming to foster regional value chains and innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By incentivizing high-skilled employment and removing trade barriers, the reform is expected to enhance regional productivity and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been enacted and is currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant adjustment to existing subsidy mechanisms, targeting high-skilled employment and regional trade, which can positively impact innovation.
reforms.source_quote: A 2022 reform is welcome as it links wage subsidies to high-skilled employment and removes the eligibility condition of a minimum distance for trade in goods and services.

reforms.description: The relaxation of eligibility conditions for investment subsidies for climate-friendly and research-intensive investments after the 2022 reform of the GRW is aimed at enhancing regional innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By supporting climate-friendly and research-intensive investments, the reform is expected to stimulate innovation and productivity in targeted regions.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been enacted and is currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform modifies existing funding mechanisms to better align with climate goals and innovation, representing a significant but not structural change.
reforms.source_quote: The relaxation of eligibility conditions for investment subsidies for climate-friendly and research-intensive investments after the 2022 reform of the GRW are welcome.

reforms.description: Germany is implementing a significant increase in R&D support aimed at fostering the development of new green technologies, including green hydrogen, to facilitate emission reductions in high-cost sectors like steel and cement production.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This initiative is expected to enhance productivity and innovation in green technologies, contributing to long-term economic growth and environmental sustainability.
reforms.status_evidence: The government plans to allocate up to EUR 50 billion for carbon contracts for difference schemes over the next 15 years.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a major shift in Germany's industrial policy towards sustainable practices, with substantial funding aimed at transforming key sectors and addressing climate change.
reforms.source_quote: R&D support is provided to foster the development of new green technologies, for example green hydrogen, and facilitate emission reductions in sectors with high abatement costs.

reforms.description: The government plans to phase out renewable energy subsidies to free resources for more targeted support for green R&D, aiming to improve the cost-effectiveness of low-carbon technologies and stimulate research in green innovations.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Redirecting funds from broad subsidies to targeted R&D can enhance innovation in green technologies, leading to long-term cost reductions and environmental benefits.
reforms.status_evidence: The government plans to phase out subsidies to renewable energy producers.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in funding strategy, focusing on R&D for green technologies, which is crucial for achieving climate goals and fostering innovation.
reforms.source_quote: Renewable energy subsidies should be phased out to free resources for more targeted support for green R&D.

reforms.description: The government has simplified planning and approval procedures for electricity grid expansion by classifying it as a project of overriding national interest, which accelerates permit approvals.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Streamlining grid expansion processes enhances infrastructure development, which is essential for supporting renewable energy integration and overall economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has simplified planning and approval procedures for grid expansion.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform significantly improves the efficiency of infrastructure development, which is vital for the energy transition, but does not represent a complete overhaul of the system.
reforms.source_quote: The government has simplified planning and approval procedures by classifying the electricity grid expansion as a project with overriding national interest.

reforms.description: The federal government plans to facilitate the commercialisation of research by allowing universities to engage more closely with venture capital funds and banks, promoting the development of proof-of-concept funds for academic spin-offs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing university engagement with venture capital will facilitate the transfer of research into marketable products, thus driving innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government plans to allow universities to engage more closely with venture capital funds.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative is significant as it aims to bridge the gap between research and market application, enhancing the innovation ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: The federal government should also review the regulatory framework to allow universities to engage more closely with venture capital funds.

reforms.description: The allocation of about EUR 760 million in broadband support through the GFS programme aims to improve connectivity in lagging regions, addressing market failures that lead to underinvestment in high-speed broadband capacity.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving broadband connectivity enhances digital technology adoption by firms, fostering regional development and attracting skilled workers.
reforms.status_evidence: The allocation of funds for broadband support has been confirmed.
reforms.importance_rationale: This funding represents a significant investment in regional infrastructure, which is crucial for enhancing digital connectivity and supporting economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The allocation of about EUR 760 million in broadband support through the GFS programme to lagging regions... is a welcome step.

reforms.description: The newly introduced refundable R&D tax credit could be expanded to include R&D-related capital expenditure, which currently does not apply to software, licenses, and IP rights used for R&D.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Expanding the R&D tax credit to include capital expenditures will incentivize firms to invest more in R&D, thereby enhancing innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The expansion of the R&D tax credit has been proposed but not yet legislated.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant adjustment to the existing tax credit framework, potentially increasing R&D investments by firms.
reforms.source_quote: The newly introduced refundable R&D tax credit could be expanded to include R&D-related capital expenditure.

reforms.description: Germany has introduced a refundable R&D tax credit that subsidises 25% and 35% of up to EUR 10 million of business expenditure on R&D per year for large firms and SMEs, respectively, to improve conditions for firms investing in knowledge-based capital.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This reform incentivizes private sector investment in R&D, which is crucial for innovation and productivity growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The refundable R&D tax credit has been amended to support business expenditure on R&D.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in the R&D funding landscape, enhancing the capacity of firms to invest in innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The refundable R&D tax credit subsidises 25% and 35% of up to EUR 10 million of business expenditure on R&D per year for large firms and SMEs.

reforms.description: Recent reforms have simplified the application procedures of the Central Innovation Programme for SMEs (ZIM), which supports SMEs in their digital transformation and fosters collaboration with research institutions.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating access to support for digital transformation, this reform enhances the innovation capacity of SMEs, which is vital for overall economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The application procedures of the Central Innovation Programme for SMEs have been simplified.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform significantly improves the accessibility of support for SMEs, which is crucial for enhancing their innovation capabilities.
reforms.source_quote: Recent reforms have partly simplified the time-consuming and complex application procedures of the Central Innovation Programme for SMEs.
